_comment: Hand-maintained. This is the ONLY file where humans/agents record known issues per capability. Do not duplicate this information in prose docs — STATUS.md is generated FROM this file plus recon_findings.json, so editing STATUS.md directly will be overwritten. Capability names must match keys in recon_findings.json exactly.

_schema.capability: must match a key in recon_findings.json capabilities{}
_schema.detail_ref: path#anchor to the full writeup, e.g. docs/adr/0003-xxx.md or docs/handoff/2026-07-03-audit-findings.md
_schema.blocks_goal: true if this blocks the project's primary use case (agent-driven strategy iteration)

issues.summary: Returns raw dialog text split by newline, not structured fields. No selector to target individual inputs.
Returns unparsed raw text blob instead of key/value pairs.
Uses guessed generic selectors (input[name=], [data-name=], placeholder match) never confirmed against real TradingView Inputs dialog DOM. Will likely silently fail to match.
Fixed: now delegates to DomPineScriptBackend.write (clipboard-based, per ADR-0002) instead of broken ta.value=. Marked verified:false pending live-session test per ADR-0003.

Dead end on both implemented paths. DOM backend punts to network path with a CapabilityUnavailable; network backend's get_ohlcv also unconditionally raises CapabilityUnavailable despite its own docstring claiming OHLCV is the one thing the network path supports. No working OHLCV read exists.
